disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Spontaneous resolution at 12 months: ~50-70% of patients; persistent symptoms at 2 years in 30-50%; severely debilitating long COVID in 10-15% at 1 year without intervention
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: No approved pharmacotherapy; Paxlovid (nirmatrelvir/ritonavir) — PANORAMIC trial showed no reduction in Long COVID risk at 6 months if started in acute phase; BC007 (autoantibody neutraliser) — Phase 2 signal; low-dose naltrexone — observational data positive; antihistamine combination (famotidine + cetirizine) — anecdotal
remission.gap_size: Very large — no proven pharmacotherapy; enormous research investment but no approved treatment as of mid-2026
remission.barrier_type: biological (mechanism not fully understood) + practice (no evidence-based protocol)
remission.barrier_detail: Long COVID is a heterogeneous syndrome with multiple proposed mechanisms (viral persistence, autoimmunity, microclotting, dysautonomia, mitochondrial dysfunction); no single treatment works for all patients; fragmented care with poor coordination; many clinicians still do not acknowledge Long COVID as a diagnosis
remission.last_soc_change: 2024 — NIH RECOVER initiative released clinical trial protocols for LDN, anticoagulation, anti-viral, and SSRIs; no approved treatment exists; WHO case definition 2021; CDC guidance 2022
remission.outdated_treatments: Advising rest alone (worsens deconditioning without addressing underlying mechanisms); graded exercise therapy (as in ME/CFS — potentially harmful in post-exertional malaise phenotype); dismissing symptoms as psychosomatic without investigation of autonomic function, inflammatory markers, microclot testing
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Pacing (heart rate-based, matching POTS/dysautonomia protocols); H1+H2 antihistamine combination (famotidine + loratadine/cetirizine — anecdotal but mechanistically plausible for mast cell activation); low-dose naltrexone (LDN); BC007 (G-protein-coupled receptor autoantibody neutralisation); ivermectin (ongoing controversy — TOGETHER trial no benefit for acute, sparse Long COVID data); stellate ganglion block for POTS/dysautonomia subtype
remission.frontier_signal: BC007 (G-PCRAB neutralisation — Phase 2); AXA1125 (amino acid EAA for mitochondrial support); RSLV-132 (RNase-Fc fusion for RNA-containing immune complexes); Paxlovid for Long COVID viral persistence hypothesis (PTRB trial); SSRI/SNRI for serotonin depletion hypothesis (RECOVER-VITAL trial); anticoagulation/fibrinolysis for microclot hypothesis
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
